rootCauseAnalysis.summary: Deployment api-gateway was patched to reference a broken ConfigMap (gateway-config-bad) containing an unsupported 'invalid_directive: true' field, causing all updated pods to abort at config-parse time with exit code 1 and enter CrashLoopBackOff. The valid ConfigMap (gateway-config) exists in the same namespace and is the correct fix target.
causalChain: Signal: Pod api-gateway-5b4ccdb7df-4w56v is CrashLoopBackOff (4 restarts, exit code 1, runs ~1 second)
Why? Container logs show '[emerg] invalid directive found in /etc/demo-http-server/config.yaml — aborting' — application aborts at startup during config parsing
Why? The config file at /etc/demo-http-server/config.yaml is sourced from ConfigMap gateway-config-bad, which contains 'invalid_directive: true' — an unsupported field the server rejects

Why? The Deployment api-gateway was patched at 18:20:37Z to change its volume ConfigMap reference from gateway-config (valid) to gateway-config-bad (invalid) — a misconfiguration introduced post-deployment

Root cause: Deployment api-gateway spec references ConfigMap gateway-config-bad instead of the valid ConfigMap gateway-config, injecting a malformed config file that aborts the application on every start

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting Deployment/api-gateway because its spec.template.spec.volumes[config].configMap.name needs to be changed from gateway-config-bad back to gateway-config. The pod is the symptom reporter; the Deployment is the misconfigured resource. An automated workflow can patch this single field to resolve the issue.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Single Deployment affected. The fix (patch volume ConfigMap name) is minimal and surgical. The 4 old replicas using gateway-config are healthy, confirming the application itself is not broken.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: pod (describe, logs, previous logs, events), Deployment (spec, status, managedFields), both ConfigMaps (gateway-config and gateway-config-bad), ReplicaSet ownership chain. The Deployment shows 4 ready replicas on the old ReplicaSet using gateway-config, and 3 unavailable on the new ReplicaSet using gateway-config-bad, confirming the scope is limited to the new rollout. Node was implicitly validated as healthy via successful scheduling and image presence events.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: CrashLoopBackOff → exit code 1 → config parse error in logs → invalid_directive in mounted ConfigMap → Deployment volume reference pointing to gateway-config-bad. The patch timestamp and manager (kubectl-patch) in managedFields confirm this was a deliberate but erroneous config change. Cannot go deeper without access to who issued the kubectl patch command.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims are backed by tool output: (1) kubectl_describe confirmed ConfigMap gateway-config-bad is mounted, (2) kubectl_logs and kubectl_previous_logs both show the exact abort message, (3) kubectl_get_by_name retrieved gateway-config-bad contents showing invalid_directive: true, (4) kubectl_find_resource confirmed gateway-config exists and is valid, (5) Deployment managedFields show a kubectl-patch operation changed the ConfigMap reference at 18:20:37Z. No inaccessible data sources.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for Deployment/api-gateway (tier1 and tier2 history both empty).
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: OOMKill: ruled out — memory limit is 64Mi and the container exits in 1 second before allocating significant memory; no OOMKilled reason in pod status. Image pull failure: ruled out — events show 'Container image already present on machine'. Node pressure/eviction: ruled out — pod was successfully scheduled and started; no eviction events. Liveness probe killing the container: ruled out — container dies before the 3-second initialDelaySeconds of the liveness probe fires. Application bug: ruled out — the same image runs successfully on the 4 pods still using gateway-config.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: Starting at 1.0. -0.02 for not verifying who issued the kubectl-patch (audit log not accessible). -0.01 for not confirming whether the 3 unavailable pods are all from the same ReplicaSet (high confidence based on Deployment rollout status but not explicitly verified per-pod). Final confidence: 0.97.

contributingFactors: Deployment volume reference was patched from gateway-config (valid) to gateway-config-bad (invalid) at 2026-04-27T18:20:37Z — 27 seconds after initial creation, likely an erroneous kubectl-patch during a test scenario
ConfigMap gateway-config-bad contains an unsupported 'invalid_directive: true' field that the demo-http-server rejects at startup
Container exits in ~1 second with exit code 1 — config-parse abort before any network listener is opened
3 updated replicas (all mounting gateway-config-bad) are unavailable; only 4 old replicas remain ready out of 5 desired
Valid ConfigMap gateway-config exists in the same namespace with identical routing config minus the invalid directive

kaResponse.analysis: Deployment api-gateway was patched to reference a broken ConfigMap (gateway-config-bad) containing an unsupported 'invalid_directive: true' field, causing all updated pods to abort at config-parse time with exit code 1 and enter CrashLoopBackOff. The valid ConfigMap (gateway-config) exists in the same namespace and is the correct fix target.
